name: Vara del Rey and El Cañavate
name_original: Vara del Rey y El Cañavate — circular desde San Clemente
description: Cycle 48.1 miles (77.5 km) on a moderate route from San Clemente, exploring the La Mancha plains and historic towns like Vara del Rey.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around San Clemente are characterized by a diverse landscape that includes coastal paths, canyons, and ridgelines. The area features a mix of terrain, from flat coastal stretches to challenging routes with significant elevation gains. This geography provides varied options for cyclists, with sandstone bluffs and coastal views along the Pacific Ocean. Inland, the landscape transitions to hills and coastal canyons, offering panoramic mountain-to-sea vistas. Best road cycling routes around San Clemente The most popular road cycling route is Castillo de Belmonte — circular por la Alberca de Záncara, a 38.0 miles (61.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 45 minutes to complete. This moderate route features an elevation gain of 1,457 feet (444 meters). Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Vara del Rey and El Cañavate, a moderate 48.1 miles (77.5 km) path. This route includes an elevation gain of 1,359 feet (414 meters) and can be completed in about 3 hours 20 minutes. Local road cyclists also love the Villarrobledo – Socuéllamos Town Center loop from Bodegon el Abuelo, a 33.7 miles (54.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 20 minutes. Road cycling around San Clemente is defined by coastal paths, canyons, and ridgelines. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal rides to more challenging routes with significant climbs. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 20 road cyclists have used komoot to explore San Clemente's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around San Clemente?
A: The road cycling routes around San Clemente offer a diverse landscape, featuring a mix of coastal paths, canyons, and ridgelines. You'll find everything from flat coastal stretches to more challenging routes with significant elevation gains, providing varied options for all skill levels.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: Yes, there are routes suitable for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31843978" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañada Juncosa – El Cañavate loop from Cañada Juncosa&lt;/a&gt; is classified as an easy route, covering approximately 18.7 kilometers with a moderate elevation gain, making it a good option for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What are some popular road cycling routes in the San Clemente area?
A: One of the most popular road cycling routes is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1110629036/castillo-de-belmonte-circular-por-la-alberca-de-zancara" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Belmonte — circular por la Alberca de Záncara&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 61.1 km trail. Another favorite among local cyclists is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103943052/vara-del-rey-and-el-canavate" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vara del Rey and El Cañavate&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 77.5 km path.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in San Clemente?
A: There are several road cycling routes available around San Clemente, with komoot featuring at least 3 distinct routes that cater to various preferences and skill levels.

Q: What is the typical duration for road cycling routes in this region?
A: The duration of road cycling routes in San Clemente varies depending on the route's length and your pace. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1110629036/castillo-de-belmonte-circular-por-la-alberca-de-zancara" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Belmonte — circular por la Alberca de Záncara&lt;/a&gt; route typically takes about 2 hours and 45 minutes, while &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103943052/vara-del-rey-and-el-canavate" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vara del Rey and El Cañavate&lt;/a&gt; can take around 3 hours and 20 minutes.

Q: Are there any loop routes for road cycling around San Clemente?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops, offering a convenient way to start and end your ride in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167187" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villarrobledo – Socuéllamos Town Center loop from Bodegon el Abuelo&lt;/a&gt;, a 54.2 km trail.

Q: What kind of elevation gain can I expect on these routes?
A: Elevation gains vary significantly. Moderate routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1110629036/castillo-de-belmonte-circular-por-la-alberca-de-zancara" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Belmonte — circular por la Alberca de Záncara&lt;/a&gt; feature an elevation gain of around 444 meters, while easier routes will have less, and more challenging ones could have more substantial climbs.

Q: What attractions can I see near the road cycling routes in San Clemente?
A: While cycling, you can explore several historical and cultural points of interest. These include the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2519698" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Santiago de la Torre&lt;/a&gt;, the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5454480" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor of San Clemente&lt;/a&gt;, and unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5454612" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Typical Manchegan houses&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass through the settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5891279" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Provencio&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in San Clemente?
A: Road cyclists using komoot often praise the varied terrain, which includes coastal paths, canyons, and ridgelines, offering options for different ability levels. The region's diverse landscape and well-maintained routes are frequently highlighted as key attractions.

Q: Are there any specific trails or promenades suitable for cycling?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5892213" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Záncara Promenade&lt;/a&gt; is a notable trail in the area that can be incorporated into or enjoyed alongside road cycling routes, offering a pleasant experience.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in San Clemente?
A: San Clemente generally offers a pleasant climate for cycling. With its mild weather, road cycling is enjoyable for much of the year, though specific seasonal considerations might apply depending on your preference for temperature and wind conditions.

Cuenca town on the banks of the Rus River. Vestiges of the Bronze Age have been found, which detonates the antiquity of these populated areas.
You can visit the church with Romanesque, Gothic and Renaissance elements and the hermitage of Ermita de la Virgen del Trascastillo, from the 16th century.

San Clemente (Cuenca) is an ideal destination for those who enjoy history, heritage, and peaceful tourism. Highly recommended as a cultural getaway.
Very well-preserved historical heritage
One of its main attractions is the Old Tower, the oldest building in the town, dating back to the 14th century. It stands out for its square floor plan, battlements, gargoyles, and historical significance. Inside, you'll find the Tourist Office and the Ethnographic Museum of Farming, with antique objects that illustrate the traditional way of life in the area.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is designed for road cycling and typically takes around 3 hours and 20 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your cycling pace and any stops you make along the way.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers an average road biking experience with a mainly well-paved and easily navigable surface, making it suitable for road bikes. You can expect some initial gentle ascents followed by extensive plains, characteristic of the La Mancha landscape.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: The route is considered an 'average road bike ride' and requires good fitness. While the terrain is mainly well-paved, its distance of nearly 77.5 km and moderate elevation gain might be challenging for absolute beginners. Cyclists with some experience and good fitness will find it enjoyable.

faqs.question: Where does the Vara del Rey y El Cañavate circular route start, and is parking available?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in San Clemente. While specific parking details aren't provided, San Clemente is a town, so public parking options should be available. Additionally, the starting point is accessible by public transport.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route takes you through the rural landscapes of La Mancha, offering views of extensive plains. You'll pass through the settlements of Vara del Rey and El Cañavate. The starting and ending point, San Clemente, is a historically rich town with a well-preserved Roman bridge, palaces, convents, and museums.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given its location in the province of Cuenca, Spain, the best times to cycle this route are typically spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, temperatures are generally milder and more comfortable for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer or the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public road route.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is primarily a road cycling tour on public roads. While there are no explicit restrictions, bringing dogs on a 77.5 km road cycling trip might not be practical or safe for most pets. If you do bring a dog, ensure it is safely secured and consider its comfort and endurance for such a distance.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of the Ruta del Cristal de Hispania, Camino de Santiago de Levante, Camino de Santiago del Sureste, and Camino Belmonte.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect on this ride?
faqs.answer: You can expect to see the characteristic La Mancha landscape, which includes extensive plains and rural scenery. The route offers a pleasant cycling experience through agricultural areas, with the historical town of San Clemente providing cultural interest at the start and end.
